title: openai-responses provider: 404 on previous_response_id when store=false (default)
author: xneone
body:
## Bug Description

When using a provider configured with api: openai-responses, the gateway sends store=false in responses API requests. On subsequent turns within the same session, the gateway includes previous_response_id referencing the previous response. Since that response was never persisted server-side, the API returns a 404.

HTTP 404: Item with id rs_xxxxx not found. Items are not persisted when store is set to false. Try again with store set to true, or remove this item from your input.

## Expected Behavior

The provider should either avoid previous_response_id when store=false or persist the response when continuing a conversation.

Related regressions were discussed in #84904 and #87310.

activity.author: clawsweeper
activity.body: Codex review: keeping this open for maintainer follow-up. Current main still has a plausible Responses replay failure for custom openai-responses providers because generic Responses payloads can be unstored while replaying server-owned reasoning item IDs.

activity.author: tianxiaochannel-oss88
activity.body: Adding a fresh macOS/Slack data point: the same previous_response_id 404 appears after the second message when store=false is used through the openai-responses provider. Removing the previous response reference makes the next request succeed.

activity.author: osolmaz
activity.body: The fix should preserve normal stored-response continuation while disabling replay through missing server-side response IDs for store=false sessions.

warnings: fixture warning: public REST timeline evidence is deterministic for capture validation
